Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients
Ticket number: TI-1624175
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 25
Owner: Employee 30
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 10.12.25 08:41
Closure
Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04
Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored.
Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.
Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally.
Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.
Test scan to external address → no delivery.
No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?
10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response
Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.
Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location.
11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay
• Relay allows only defined IP ranges
• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed
12.12.25 – Further analysis
Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team
Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list.
16.12.25 – Adjustment
SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.
17.12.25 – Test phase
Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: The statement begins with a direct instruction to 'Verify the printer’s IP and VLAN,' which is an explicit recommendation/action.
groundedness_reason: The ticket log documents checking the printer IP, finding it in a newly defined printer VLAN, and confirming that this VLAN was not included in the SMTP relay allow list (entries on 12.12.25 and 15.12.25), matching both parts of the answer.

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue
Ticket number: TI-1623488
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 20
Owner: Employee 27
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 02.12.25 09:03
Closure
Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21
Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again.
Activities
02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report
Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents.
All other employees in the same room can print without issue.
The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print.
02.12.25 09:03
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27.
02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with the affected user.
Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible.
When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately.
Print job is not displayed in the queue.
02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user
Test performed with colleague in the same office.
Printing works without restriction.
User account checked.
Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed.
02.12.25 10:30 – Further review
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
It specifies:
• Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups
• No individual user permissions at queue level
• NTFS and share permissions must be consistent
Review of the printer queue on the print server shows:
• AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission
• Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny”
This entry originates from a previous test configuration.
02.12.25 11:10 – Test
Explicit deny permission removed.
User logged in again.
Test print successful.
03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback
Printing works normally again.
Additional tests within the team successful.
03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 20
The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user.
The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Printing is now fully functional again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: The statement gives conditional, directive instructions (e.g., 'If an explicit Deny is present, remove it', 'ensure permissions', 'have the user log off and log on') which constitute recommendations.
groundedness_reason: The ticket shows an explicit 'Deny' on the printer queue was removed, permissions were cleaned up to use AD groups per the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard, and the user logged off/on and printing then succeeded, directly supporting the recommended steps.

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default
Ticket number: TI-1623017
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 18
Owner: Employee 23
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.11.25 08:49
Closure
Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11
Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable.
Activities
18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location.
They must manually select the correct printer before each print job.
The issue seems to have started last week.
Kind regards
18.11.25 08:49
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23.
18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with an affected user.
Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”.
Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list.
Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set.
18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department?
Kind regards
18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response
Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week.
The old group was deactivated.
19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that:
• Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups
• Default printers per location are set via GPO
• No parallel groups with identical function may exist
Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group.
As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default.
19.11.25 11:30 – Test
Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership.
gpupdate /force executed.
User logged in again.
Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”.
20.11.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization.
Correction performed according to blueprint specification.
24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback
Multiple users tested.
Default printer remains correct.
No further automatic switching.
24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment.
Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: The statement opens with an explicit directive to 'Confirm the user’s AD group memberships and compare to the expected group(s),' which clearly advises an action to be taken.
groundedness_reason: Ticket TI-1623017 documents the Finance team being moved into a new AD group that was additionally a member of a Zurich location group, causing the Zurich printer to become the default until the incorrect group membership was removed and the group structure cleaned up.

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue
Ticket number: TI-1623488
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 20
Owner: Employee 27
Origin: Phone

Closure
Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21
Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again.
Activities
02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report
Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents.
All other employees in the same room can print without issue.
The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print.
02.12.25 09:03
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27.
02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with the affected user.
Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible.
When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately.
Print job is not displayed in the queue.
02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user
Test performed with colleague in the same office.
Printing works without restriction.
User account checked.
Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed.
02.12.25 10:30 – Further review
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
It specifies:
• Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups
• No individual user permissions at queue level
• NTFS and share permissions must be consistent
Review of the printer queue on the print server shows:
• AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission
• Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny”
This entry originates from a previous test configuration.
02.12.25 11:10 – Test
Explicit deny permission removed.
User logged in again.
Test print successful.
03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback
Printing works normally again.
Additional tests within the team successful.
03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 20
The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user.
The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Printing is now fully functional again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default
Ticket number: TI-1623017
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 18
Owner: Employee 23
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.11.25 08:49
Closure
Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11
Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable.
Activities
18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location.
They must manually select the correct printer before each print job.
The issue seems to have started last week.
Kind regards
18.11.25 08:49
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23.
18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with an affected user.
Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”.
Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list.
Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set.
18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department?
Kind regards
18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response
Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week.
The old group was deactivated.
19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that:
• Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups
• Default printers per location are set via GPO
• No parallel groups with identical function may exist
Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group.
As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default.
19.11.25 11:30 – Test
Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership.
gpupdate /force executed.
User logged in again.
Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”.
20.11.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization.
Correction performed according to blueprint specification.
24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback
Multiple users tested.
Default printer remains correct.
No further automatic switching.
24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment.
Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients
Ticket number: TI-1624175
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 25
Owner: Employee 30
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 10.12.25 08:41
Closure
Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04
Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored.
Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.
Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally.
Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.
Test scan to external address → no delivery.
No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?
10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response
Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.
Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location.
11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay
• Relay allows only defined IP ranges
• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed
12.12.25 – Further analysis
Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team
Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list.
16.12.25 – Adjustment
SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.
17.12.25 – Test phase
Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

Ticket number: TI-1571046

Activities
04.03.25 13:18
Ticket created via customer portal
Description:
Several employees report that after being added to the project “ERP-Upgrade”, they generally have access to SharePoint, but the folder
/Projects/ERP_Upgrade/Finance
is not displayed.
In some cases, access works for certain users after logging out and back in, but not for others.
Error message:
Access denied or item not found
04.03.25 13:32
Ticket assigned to: Employee 21
Status: In Progress
Notes:
04.03.25 14:05 – Initial analysis
• Affected users are members of Azure AD group “ERP_Finance”
• This group is a member of “SP_ERP_Project”
• SharePoint permissions correctly configured
• Comparison with working user shows identical group structure
Test:
Direct access via URL → partially successful
Notable:

04.03.25 15:40 – Conditional Access review

05.03.25 09:10 – Token analysis

• Multiple entries with “Conditional Access not satisfied”

Users who had not fully logged out were working with cached tokens.

• User fully logged out (Office + browser)
• Browser cache cleared
• Device compliance status checked

Access possible afterward.
06.03.25 11:50 – In-depth analysis

These users access via Hybrid Azure AD Joined Devices.
Device synchronization was faulty.

→ SharePoint project folder no longer included in Finance-specific CA rule
• Users re-authenticated

Sign-In Logs unremarkable.
11.03.25 16:02 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 05
The root cause was not group nesting, but a delay in Conditional Access policy evaluation combined with cached authentication tokens.
After policy adjustment and renewed authentication, access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Subject: Access to Compliance_Review folder missing for some users
From: Contact Person 02
Good morning
As part of our internal audit, we have identified that some employees do not have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Others can see it without issue.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 02
18.03.25 08:43
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 27
Status: In Progress
Notes
18.03.25 09:15
Helpdesk asks:
• Which users exactly are affected?
• Since when?
• Screenshot of error message?
• Access via browser or Teams?
18.03.25 10:02
Customer responds:
Affected users:
• M. Keller
• S. Braun
• T. Widmer
Error message:
Item not found
Access via browser (Edge).
Noticed since last week, but unclear exactly since when.
18.03.25 10:25
Helpdesk asks further:
• Are users members of the project group?
• Were permissions recently changed?
• Does it affect only this folder or others as well?
18.03.25 11:18
Customer:
We restructured several project groups last week.
New groups were created:
• COMPLIANCE_TEAM
• COMPLIANCE_EXT
Folder may have been newly inherited.
Other folders work.
18.03.25 13:05
Helpdesk checks SharePoint permissions:
• Folder has Unique Permissions
• Permission via group “SP_Compliance_Main”

Test with working user → access OK
Test with affected user → no access
19.03.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are the users possibly assigned via multiple groups?
Were there recent role changes (HR)?
19.03.25 10:40
Customer responds:
Two users changed departments internally (Finance → Risk).
One works 50% in both teams.
Could that be relevant?

Helpdesk checks group memberships:
All three users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”.
No obvious differences.
19.03.25 14:55
Customer reports:
One user had access briefly today, then not again.
After restart it did not work.
20.03.25 08:20
Helpdesk asks:
Are users accessing from different devices?
Company laptop or private device?
With or without VPN?
20.03.25 09:03
Customer:
Two via company laptop
One via private device
VPN not required (cloud-only environment).
20.03.25 10:45
Helpdesk checks:
• Clear browser cache
• Test in Incognito mode
• Test in another browser
Result:

Customer:
After signing out of all Office apps, it worked briefly.
Later not visible again.

Was the folder possibly moved or renamed?
Was the Sensitivity Label changed?
Is the document library fully loaded?

Customer:
Folder was renamed 2 weeks ago from “Compliance_Audit” to “Compliance_Review_2025”.
Sensitivity Label “Confidential – Internal” applied.
21.03.25 13:30
Helpdesk checks:
Label requires:
• Encryption
• Access only for group “Compliance_Core”
Notable:
“Compliance_Core” is not identical to “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”.
However, according to SharePoint, permission is still managed via “SP_Compliance_Main”.
Unclear correlation.

Ticket remains open due to inconsistent behavior.
Status: In Progress
22.03.25 09:05
Internal note – Employee 27
Since the behavior remains inconsistent (partial access, partial denial, partial access only in Incognito mode), the ticket is escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – M365 / Identity
22.03.25 09:12
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 11
Status: In Progress
22.03.25 10:25
Analysis by System Engineer 11
Step 1 – Sensitivity Label review
Label “Confidential – Internal” checked:
• Encryption active
• Access only for members of Azure AD group “Compliance_Core”

Folder “Compliance_Review_2025” has Unique Permissions.

Hypothesis: Conflict between SharePoint permissions and AIP label encryption.
22.03.25 11:40
Test:
• User added directly to “Compliance_Core”
→ Access immediately possible
After removal from “Compliance_Core”
→ Access denied again
However:
Other users WITHOUT “Compliance_Core” membership have access.
Hypothesis rejected – not purely group-based.

• No claim for “Compliance_Core”

No blocking visible.
24.03.25 10:10
In-depth review via Microsoft Purview / Information Protection
is_rec_reason: The sentence uses an imperative directive ('Review Azure AD Sign‑In Logs...') instructing an action to examine logs, which constitutes a recommendation.
groundedness_reason: The ticket's Azure AD Sign‑In Logs analysis explicitly states tokens for affected users contained a claim for "COMPLIANCE_TEAM" and no claim for "Compliance_Core", which directly supports both the recommendation to review sign-in logs and the observed token contents.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Duplicate groups / synchronization issues (external users sometimes having and sometimes losing access; folder invisible in list but reachable by URL can also point to group/object ID mismatches or nesting)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 13:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: Describes observed symptoms and possible causes (diagnosis) without offering directives, recommendations, or suggested actions.
groundedness_reason: The ticket (KF25) documents two identically named groups (cloud vs. on‑prem) and Azure AD Connect synchronization overwriting the cloud group, causing external users to intermittently lose access, supporting the duplicate-group/synchronization claim. However, the context does not mention a folder being invisible in a list but reachable by URL, so that specific symptom and its link to nesting/object‑ID mismatches is not supported.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Confirm whether the folder has Unique Permissions and which SharePoint group/object id is referenced in the ACL

was not activated.
Result:
• Existing SharePoint permissions formally remained
• Encryption, however, applies only to explicitly defined groups
This creates a situation where:
SharePoint says → Access allowed
AIP encryption says → Access only with key permission
Depending on token refresh timing, access is granted or blocked.

The folder was:
1. Renamed
2. Assigned Unique Permissions
3. Subsequently encrypted with a Sensitivity Label
The encryption defined a different permission group than the SharePoint ACL.
Token caching caused inconsistent behavior.
25.03.25 11:40 – Actions
• Sensitivity Label removed
• Folder reassigned with correct group reference
• Label reapplied with correct group integration
• “Compliance_Core” and “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” structurally aligned
• Tests performed with multiple user accounts
• Tokens intentionally invalidated

Multiple users tested
Access consistently possible
No further error messages

Audit team confirms stable access for all employees.

The root cause was an inconsistent combination of Unique SharePoint permissions and a subsequently applied Sensitivity Label encryption.
Due to differing group references between ACL and encryption, inconsistent access behavior occurred, amplified by token caching.
The permission structure was aligned and tested.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Hi,

The "loop"
is_rec_reason: This statement reports a root cause and documents the remediation that was performed and its outcome, without recommending or advising any future action.

The MAM policy correctly enforced that work data could not be accessed on non-compliant devices, preventing email setup from completing.
The "loop"

SOURCE 50:
 you mention Conditional Access: as part of the migration baseline a CA policy was enabled requiring MFA for the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group. Could that be the issue?

31.01.25 16:34 Ticket closed | TI-1700004
Closure description:
Root cause was twofold. First, the service accounts were placed in the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group, which is the group targeted by the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy "KF70-Require-MFA". The Conditional Access evaluation was therefore being applied to non-interactive logons for service accounts, breaking scheduled-task authentication where MFA cannot be satisfied. Second, the service accounts had been moved to the new OU which is filtered IN by Azure AD Connect, leading to the accounts being synchronized to Entra ID and then having a license assigned (small but non-zero impact on the on-prem authentication path because of mfaPreferred attribute conflicts).
Resolution: Created a sub-group "KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA" containing the service accounts and added it to the Conditional Access policy as an explicit exclusion. Re-evaluated AAD Connect filter to keep the on-prem service-account authentication path clean. Validated that scheduled tasks are now running successfully overnight.
Documentation note: For future migrations, service accounts must be explicitly excluded from baseline CA policies via a dedicated exclusion group, and their AAD Connect sync status should be reviewed to avoid unintended cloud-side license assignment. We have added this to the migration runbook.

SOURCE 50:
 you mention Conditional Access: as part of the migration baseline a CA policy was enabled requiring MFA for the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group. Could that be the issue?

31.01.25 16:34 Ticket closed | TI-1700004
Closure description:
Root cause was twofold. First, the service accounts were placed in the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group, which is the group targeted by the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy "KF70-Require-MFA". The Conditional Access evaluation was therefore being applied to non-interactive logons for service accounts, breaking scheduled-task authentication where MFA cannot be satisfied. Second, the service accounts had been moved to the new OU which is filtered IN by Azure AD Connect, leading to the accounts being synchronized to Entra ID and then having a license assigned (small but non-zero impact on the on-prem authentication path because of mfaPreferred attribute conflicts).
Resolution: Created a sub-group "KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA" containing the service accounts and added it to the Conditional Access policy as an explicit exclusion. Re-evaluated AAD Connect filter to keep the on-prem service-account authentication path clean. Validated that scheduled tasks are now running successfully overnight.
Documentation note: For future migrations, service accounts must be explicitly excluded from baseline CA policies via a dedicated exclusion group, and their AAD Connect sync status should be reviewed to avoid unintended cloud-side license assignment. We have added this to the migration runbook.

SOURCE 50:
 you mention Conditional Access: as part of the migration baseline a CA policy was enabled requiring MFA for the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group. Could that be the issue?

31.01.25 16:34 Ticket closed | TI-1700004
Closure description:
Root cause was twofold. First, the service accounts were placed in the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group, which is the group targeted by the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy "KF70-Require-MFA". The Conditional Access evaluation was therefore being applied to non-interactive logons for service accounts, breaking scheduled-task authentication where MFA cannot be satisfied. Second, the service accounts had been moved to the new OU which is filtered IN by Azure AD Connect, leading to the accounts being synchronized to Entra ID and then having a license assigned (small but non-zero impact on the on-prem authentication path because of mfaPreferred attribute conflicts).
Resolution: Created a sub-group "KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA" containing the service accounts and added it to the Conditional Access policy as an explicit exclusion. Re-evaluated AAD Connect filter to keep the on-prem service-account authentication path clean. Validated that scheduled tasks are now running successfully overnight.
Documentation note: For future migrations, service accounts must be explicitly excluded from baseline CA policies via a dedicated exclusion group, and their AAD Connect sync status should be reviewed to avoid unintended cloud-side license assignment. We have added this to the migration runbook.
